old_claim.note: Glass-Blowing Workshops provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped late Roman glass lamps were blown glass vessels, so blown-glass forming is the direct production method.

source_shape.source_locator: The glass-lamp section says Roman glass lamps appeared during the fourth century AD and that glass lamps were blown.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Late Roman glass lamps are directly tied to blown-glass production, not merely to the broad existence of workshop organization.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the node is now scoped to early glass oil-lamp vessels, a source saying those lamps were blown licenses a direct required edge to blown_glass.

ontology_before: Early Glass Lamp Covers depended on Glass-Blowing Workshops as broad production infrastructure, leaving the specific forming technique implicit.
ontology_after: Early Glass Lamps depends directly on blown_glass as the production method for the source-backed late Roman glass lamp vessels.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: glass_lamp_chimneys now depends directly on blown_glass and no longer directly depends on glass_blowing_workshops.
would_reject_if: The node is later broadened back to non-blown glass lamp covers or protective chimneys.
A stronger source shows fourth-century Roman glass lamps were usually cast or assembled without blown-glass forming.
A narrower glass_lamp_chimney node is added where workshop organization, rather than blown-glass forming, becomes the direct dependency.
short_reason: The source-backed production method is blown glass, not the broader workshop category.
